target.title: Cache cost usage date parsers
target.body:
## Summary
- cache CostUsageDateParser ISO8601DateFormatter and DateFormatter instances per thread
- preserve current time zone behavior by including the current time zone identifier in DateFormatter cache keys
- add coverage for selecting the newest monthly cost row across supported month formats

## Why
Cost usage selection can parse many date strings while building current-day, current-session, and most-recent-month snapshots. The previous path rebuilt multiple Foundation formatters for each parse attempt, which is expensive in hot refresh paths.

## Proof
Local validation, June 16, 2026 PT:

- `.build/lint-tools/bin/swiftformat Sources Tests --lint`
  - `0/1139 files require formatting`
- `.build/lint-tools/bin/swiftlint --strict`
  - `Found 0 violations, 0 serious in 1138 files`
- `swift test --filter CostUsageDecodingTests`
  - `17 tests in 1 suite passed after 0.009 seconds`
- `swift build --build-tests`
  - `Build complete! (1.29 sec)`
- `git diff --check`
  - passed with no output

Benchmark harness was temporary and is not committed. Both runs used the same guard-based benchmark test and `swift test --skip-build --filter CostUsageDateParserBenchmarkTests` for the timed pass.

| Metric | Before | After | Change |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| parse 80k date strings | 15.027595s | 3.796186s | 4.0x faster |
| parseMonth 60k month strings | 4.345667s | 1.334223s | 3.3x faster |
| test body wall | 19.373s | 5.131s | 3.8x faster |
| command real time | 20.70s | 8.41s | 2.5x faster |
| max RSS | 107,757,568 bytes | 99,926,016 bytes | 7.3% lower |
| peak memory footprint | 29,098,704 bytes | 27,919,056 bytes | 4.1% lower |
| checksum | date 162000000 / month 720000 | date 162000000 / month 720000 | unchanged |

## Risk
Low. The change keeps the same parse order and supported formats, with thread-local formatter reuse to avoid sharing mutable Foundation formatters across threads. The time zone identifier stays part of the DateFormatter cache key so a runtime time zone change can create a fresh cached formatter.
target.author: prospectore
